hero.title: Poorya Hassanzadeh
hero.subtitle: Machine Learning Researcher | Computer Vision | Deep Learning
hero.description: ML researcher specializing in computer vision and deep learning for intelligent transportation and autonomous systems. Seeking PhD positions in ML-enabled vision systems.

about.title: About
about.description: I'm Poorya Hassanzadeh, a machine learning researcher with hands-on experience building and deploying real-world AI systems. from fine-tuning computer vision models on custom datasets to designing data-efficient deep learning pipelines for IoT environments. My work spans object detection, image retrieval, anomaly detection, and intelligent IoT systems, with a growing focus on cognitively-inspired vision for autonomous driving and traffic scene understanding.  I enjoy working at the intersection of research and real deployment; where models don't just perform well in experiments, but actually run on embedded hardware in production. I'm currently seeking a PhD position where I can push the boundaries of machine learning and computer vision, contributing to research that bridges perception, cognition, and real-world intelligent systems.
about.description2: Current focus: Cognitively-inspired vision systems for traffic scene understanding, autonomous driving applications, and behavior prediction. Interested in neural architecture optimization, transfer learning, and model compression for edge deployment.

cards.title: Production Experience
cards.description: Built and deployed computer vision and IoT systems in real industrial environments, from edge hardware inference to large-scale anomaly detection pipelines.

cards.title: Research & Academic Record
cards.description: MSc thesis on deep learning for IoT security. Paper in preparation on neural-probabilistic event detection, GPA 17.40/20.

cards.title: Seeking PhD
cards.description: Ready to contribute to cutting-edge research in machine learning, computer vision, and intelligent systems. Open to PhD opportunities, collaborations, and research discussions.

education.institution: Islamic Azad University, Tabriz Branch, Iran
education.thesis: Using Deep Learning to Detect and Prevent DDoS Attacks on the Internet of Things
education.details: Proposed and implemented a deep learning framework for detecting DDoS attacks in IoT environments under limited labeled data. Developed temporal pattern modeling applicable to behavior prediction in dynamic environments.
Final Project: Computer-controlled CNC robot using Arduino, integrating hardware control with embedded software

experience.title: Machine Learning Engineer
experience.company: GUNESH BILGISAYAR SAHAND — Computer Vision Solutions

projects.name: License Plate Detection (YOLOv8)
projects.description: Fine-tuned YOLOv8 on ~5,000 images; achieved 90–95% accuracy under low-light and angled conditions. Deployed on embedded system for real-time inference, directly applicable to traffic scene analysis and autonomous driving pipelines.

projects.name: Cement Bag Counting (YOLO)
projects.description: Developed custom counter for overlapping objects using ~2,000-image dataset; achieved ~90% accuracy in cluttered environments. Applied techniques for handling occlusion and dense scenes.

projects.name: Jewelry Similarity Search (ResNet + SIFT)
projects.description: Fused deep and handcrafted features for fine-grained retrieval on ~3,000 images; achieved 93-97% Top-1/Top-5 accuracy, a 10-15% gain over single-feature baselines.

research.title: PDLO: Neural-Enhanced Probabilistic Data Lifespan Optimization
research.description: Fast and accurate IoT event detection using neural-enhanced probabilistic approaches

research.title: Face Recognition with Raspberry Pi Using a Smartphone Camera
research.description: Real-time face recognition on Raspberry Pi using a smartphone IP camera, with GPIO-based authentication output.

research.title: Machine Learning & IoT Systems Research
research.description: Conducted experimentation on data-efficient deep learning models, implemented CNN-based architectures, and explored hybrid neural-probabilistic modeling. Studied pedestrian behavior prediction using JAAD and PIE datasets for intention estimation in traffic scenes.

research.title: Comparative Study of ML Classification Methods
research.description: Benchmarked SVM, Random Forest, and Neural Networks on unified datasets; evaluated accuracy, precision, and recall metrics.

research_interests: Computer Vision: Object detection, scene understanding, pedestrian behavior prediction, vision transformers
Deep Learning: Neural architecture optimization, transfer learning, model compression, reinforcement learning
Intelligent Transportation: Cognitively-inspired models, visual attention, human-centered AI for safety

publications.title: PDLO: Neural-Enhanced Probabilistic Data Lifespan Optimization for Fast and Accurate IoT Event Detection
